questions.question: What condition occurs when multiple threads modify shared data concurrently with output depending on execution order?
What is a binary semaphore commonly called when used strictly as a locking mechanism?
What classical algorithm can be utilized to safely avoid deadlocks in resource allocation?
What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
answers: External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers
Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks
Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes
External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space

questions.question: What state does a system enter when it spends more time swapping pages than executing instructions?
What is the function of the Memory Management Unit (MMU)?
answers: To partition hard drive sectors dynamically at runtime
To clear cache registers when a context switch occurs
To allocate network sockets to background daemons
To map virtual addresses generated by the CPU to physical memory locations

questions.question: What differentiates a process from a thread?
answers: A single thread can contain multiple independent processes
Processes share file descriptors by default; threads do not
Threads run in kernel mode; processes run in user mode
A process has its own isolated address space; threads share address spaces

questions.question: What state does a process enter if it is waiting for an I/O operation to finish?
Which scheduling algorithm runs processes based on a fixed time-slice quantum rotation?
What information is tracked inside a Process Control Block (PCB)?
answers: Process state, program counter, CPU registers, and memory boundaries
Network interface card MAC address parameters
The user web browser cache profiles
